question: Modify the `Book` class to include a destructor. The destructor should print a message indicating the title of the book object that is being destroyed.

test_cases.expected:
--- Starting Robustness Test ---
Book 'The Great Gatsby' (ID: 1) constructed.
Book 'The Great Gatsby' (ID: 1) constructed.
Created book1 (ID: The Great Gatsby) and book2 (ID: The Great Gatsby) via shallow copy.
Book 'The Great Gatsby' (ID: 1) is being destroyed.
Book 'The Great Gatsby' (ID: 1) is being destroyed.
--- Robustness Test Ended (if program didn't crash before this) ---
Book 'Dynamic Book' (ID: 5) constructed.
Dynamically allocated book created.
Book 'Dynamic Book' (ID: 5) is being destroyed.

errors: The class uses a raw `char*` for string management but fails to implement a user-defined copy constructor and copy assignment operator (violating the Rule of Three/Five).
This leads to shallow copies where multiple `Book` objects share the same dynamically allocated memory for the title.
When these shallow-copied objects are destroyed, a double-free error occurs, resulting in undefined behavior and likely a program crash.
